commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c7666c785d701658de1722b4757c31a8925bab13)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes queued embedded-agent compaction so `ownsCompaction` context engines run before native harness compaction for Codex sessions and adds a focused regression test.

PR surface: Source +75, Tests +55. Total +130 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main lets the native Codex harness return before the owning context engine runs, and the real Codex bridge skips non-manual budget compaction. I did not run the live Discord reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can make the secondary Codex-native compaction path exercise the real bridge contract and add focused coverage.

target.title: Fix context-engine compaction ownership for Codex sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- keep owning context-engine compaction primary for queued budget compaction when a Codex/native harness is also selected
- run the native harness compaction only as a bounded secondary diagnostic after a successful owning context-engine compaction
- add a focused regression test for legacy Codex-native sessions with an owning context engine

Refs #90496. This addresses the live-reproduced context-engine/native-compaction breakage from the issue, but it does not claim to close every reported symptom around provider 4xxs, scheduled-session metadata, or automatic transcript rotation policy.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Budget auto-compaction can be short-circuited by the Codex app-server native path before an owning context engine compacts the session.

Real environment tested: Live Discord lane using a Convex-leased `discord` credential, gateway started from this branch, and a proof context engine registered with `ownsCompaction: true`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent a real Discord marker mention, verified the SUT bot reply, seeded the Discord channel session with Codex/native harness metadata, then invoked queued budget compaction for that session.

Evidence after fix: The live Discord run observed the marker reply and compaction returned `ok:true`, `compacted:true`, `reason:"proof-context-engine-compacted"`, with `markerWritten:true`. The secondary Codex-native result was recorded under `details.codexNativeCompaction` and did not override the primary context-engine result.

Observed result after fix: The owning context engine performed the compaction first. The Codex app-server native path remained diagnostic-only and bounded.

What was not tested: A real production Codex provider `provider_error_4xx`, scheduled/reminder stale model metadata recreation, and a new automatic transcript-rotation product policy for genuinely unrecoverable compaction.

## Regression proof

On current `origin/main` at `80f1ae6ffe`, the same live Discord proof reproduced the narrowed failure: Discord marker observed, reset clean, but budget compaction returned `compacted:false`, `reason:"codex app-server owns automatic compaction"`, and `markerWritten:false`.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed with no accepted/actionable findings.
- `CI=true pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed, 68/68.
- `git diff --check` passed.
target.author: joshavant
